issue_title: `fs_util directory save` has `--json` option which outputs stats
issue_body:
Right now, `fs_util directory save`, when successful, always outputs a single line: `digest digest-size-bytes` (e.g. `e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 0`)

It would be useful to track the number of {files, bytes} which were {ingested, uploaded}. I'm imagining output like:

```
$ fs_util --local-store-path="${HOME}/.cache/pants/lmdb_store" directory save --root=. '**'
{
  "digest_hash": "0e5d2b4f970e7933878490c852132073ac2baac53822f9f08467783132f88d4c",
  "digest_size_bytes": 79,
  "ingested_file_count": 10,
  "ingested_file_bytes": 10719,
  "uploaded_file_count": 3,
  "uploaded_file_bytes": 254
}
```

As a first cut, let's only support this when we're uploading to a remote.

To do this, I suggest we:
1. Modify `Store::ensure_remote_has_recursive` to return a `Future` of a tuple/struct/whatever containing the four new values ({ingested, uploaded} file {count, bytes}), instead of a `()`.
2. In `fs_util`, use `serde_json` to serialize a `struct` containing these values, as well as the digest info, into a string.
3. Wire up a new flag in `fs_util` to optionally output this.

issue_body:
In Keras Embedding layer, K.gather operator is used (MXNet backend use mx.sym.take and TensorFlow backend use tf.gather for implementation). However, K.gather is giving error when using MXNet backend, and this issue only occurs in Embedding layer use case, not in other use cases.
**Test Case**:
 tests/keras/layers/embeddings_test.py
**Error Message**:
`Error in operator broadcast_mul0: [13:05:25] src/operator/tensor/./elemwise_binary_broadcast_op.h:67: Check failed: l == 1 || r == 1 operands could not be broadcast together with shapes [3,2] [3]
`
This [PR](https://github.com/awslabs/keras-apache-mxnet/pull/61) fixed it and is using directly mx.sym.Embedding instead of K.gather to implement the Keras Embedding Layer. Note this fix will break original Keras code and have conflict in future merge.

issue_title: Intrinsic Function `!Join` does not work on `DependsOn` property
issue_body:
Hey Guys,

I have the following SAM configuration, which works quite well so far:

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Description: "Fancy Description"

Mappings:
  Constants:
    ServerlessService:
      Version: 0.0.1

Globals:
  # Sets global settings for ALL Lambda functions
  Function:
    Runtime: nodejs6.10
    Timeout: 30
    Handler: index.handle

Resources:
  ServerlessService:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: test
      DefinitionBody:
          'Fn::Transform':
            Name: 'AWS::Include'
            Parameters:
              Location: s3://<ServerlessService-S3-Bucket>/swagger.yml

  ServerlessServiceDefaultUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: ServerlessServicetestStage
    Properties:
      ApiStages:
        - ApiId: !Ref ServerlessService
          Stage: test
      Description: Default Serverless-Service Usage Plan
      Quota:
        Limit: 5000
        Period: MONTH
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 40
        RateLimit: 20
      UsagePlanName: serverless-service-default-plan

  ServerlessServiceFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      CodeUri: ../packages/lambda/functions/serverlessServiceFunction
      Events:
        ProxyApiRoot:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            RestApiId: !Ref ServerlessService
            Path: /path/with/{params}
            Method: POST
```

Please notice, that its only working, because I'm using the undocumented "feature" (or should I say hack?) as recommended [here](https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/32#issuecomment-286334441). 

So have a closer look at this part:

```yaml
ServerlessServiceDefaultUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: ServerlessServicetestStage
    Properties:
...
```

Note, that the Resource `ServerlessServicetestStage` seems to be newly created by SAM (or what ever) and is never specified in the yaml file. Its very unintuitive to come up with this Resource name! Its really not nice, but as long as it works I don't complain.

But here is my issue: as I want to make the stage `test` at least a bit more flexible I tried to utilize the `!Join` intrinsic function.

```yaml
  ServerlessServiceDefaultUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: !Join
      - ''
      - - 'ServerlessService'
        - 'test'
        - 'Stage'
    Properties:
    ...
```

Which fails with

```
Failed to create the changeset: Waiter ChangeSetCreateComplete failed: Waiter encountered a terminal failure
 state Status: FAILED. Reason: Template format error: DependsOn must be a string or list of strings.
```

The ultimate goal is to achieve this:

```yaml
  ServerlessServiceDefaultUsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    DependsOn: !Join
      - ''
      - - 'ServerlessService'
        - !Sub ${StageName}
        - 'Stage'
    Properties:
    ...
```

It seems that `!Join` does not work on `DependsOn`.

I have the feeling that I'm failing to do a dirty workaround of an even dirtier workaround. What am I missing? What is the cleanest way to have a parameter like this:

```yaml
Parameters:
  StageName:
    Description: API Stage Name
    Type: String
    Default: test
```

And use `!Sub ${StageName}` where a Stage Name is needed?



I read #32 and #97. Both got closed without a proper solution of the issue that a UsagePlan is depending on an APIGateway. Help is **very** appreciated.

issue_title: Consistent error message for APIClient.api_server
issue_body:
When you instantiate an `APIClient` with a custom `api_server` argument, the constructor checks if the `api_server` contains the string `://`. However, if it doesn't, the error message states "When overriding the Nylas API server address, you must include https://".

This error message is inconsistent -- it's possible to create an `APIClient` with an `api_server` value of `http://example.com`, even though that uses insecure HTTP instead of secure HTTPS. The check should be made consistent with the error message, which could mean either changing the check or changing the message.

issue_title: Replay system starts successfully but return error to web UI when being pass something other than a CDXJ
issue_body:
InterPlanetary Wayback 0.2017.07.04.0020

```
$ ipwb replay test3.warc.gz
Executing first-run procedure on provided sample data.
 * Running on http://127.0.0.1:5000/ (Press CTRL+C to quit)
```
Accessing http://127.0.0.1:5000/ in a browser produces the follow on the CLI for replay
```
Retrieving URI-Ms from test3.warc.gz
getting index file at /test3.warc.gz
string index out of range
127.0.0.1 - - [08/Jul/2017 17:11:57] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 500 -
127.0.0.1 - - [08/Jul/2017 17:11:58] "GET /serviceWorker.js HTTP/1.1" 200 -
```

and only a message that says, "Error" in the browser. Though the argument directed to an invalid source (a warc.gz.cdxj likely does not contain CDXJ data), there ought to be failsafes in-places to alert the user that the file passed to the replay system is not of the expected file type.

Further, the replay web UI ought to respond more gracefully than echo'ing the message "Error".

issue_title: Add an HTTP header to indicate and allow serviceWorker upgrading
issue_body:
In #155, @ibnesayeed recommended adding an HTTP response header to the replay system to indicate the version of the ServiceWorker, e.g., `X-ServiceWorker: serviceWorker.js:v1`.

This would:
* If the server receives a request without that header then it can decide to send a page that would install the SW
* If installation fails, a message can be shown to the user that the browser is unsupported
* If possible, server might send a rewritten response as a fallback (which we have no intent of doing in IPWB though)
* If it does receive the header, but the version is old, then it can be upgraded with new logic

A versioning scheme should be used to indicate to the replay system to refresh the SW.

issue_title: removeGaps in Reads filtering does not make the corresponding change to the quality string
issue_body:
In `reads.py`:

```python
        if self.removeGaps:
            # TODO: There's a bug here. The sequence can be shortened but
            # the quality string (if any) is not.
            sequence = read.sequence.replace('-', '')
            read = read.__class__(read.id, sequence, read.quality)
```
Should instead be something like:
```python
        if self.removeGaps:
            if read.quality is not None:
                newSequence = []
                newQuality = []
                for base, q in zip(read.sequence, read.quality):
                    if base != '-':
                        newSequence.append(base)
                        newQuality.append(q)
                read = read.__class__(read.id, ''.join(newSequence), ''.join(newQuality)
            else:
                sequence = read.sequence.replace('-', '')
                read = read.__class__(read.id, sequence, read.quality)
```

issue_title: Allow for custom logger to be passed to ApplicationSession
issue_body:
ApplicationSession does not currently allow a custom logger to be passed to the logger instance that txaio uses. It would be nice to allow a custom logger to be passed based on the standard lib logging module. This would allow for better control of a log file for administration, and not duplicate entry with no way to stop it:

``` text
[04-28-2016 15:58:32.589 UTC] PID: 1659 Mod: timer Line: 16 INFO: build_json_tree sort finished in: 0.002349 secs
2016-04-28T11:58:32 build_json_tree sort finished in: 0.002349 secs
``` 

``` python
class ApplicationSession(BaseSession):
    """
    WAMP endpoint session.
    """

    log = txaio.make_logger() or self.config.logger  # NOTE: Or something like this

    def __init__(self, config=None):
        """
        Constructor.
        """
        BaseSession.__init__(self)
        self.config = config or types.ComponentConfig(realm=u"default")
```

``` python
class SessionComponent(ApplicationSession):
    def __init__(self, config=None, hostname=None, logger=None):
        ApplicationSession.__init__(self, config, logger)
```

issue_title: allow sanitizing of custom types
issue_body:
Niket ran into an issue where he wants to include his own custom types in model output (and, in particular, in predictor output).

Unfortunately, there's no way to allow for this, except for something like manually doing serializing in `Model.decode`.

A somewhat simple fix would be to add another clause to `sanitize` that checks if an object has a `to_json()` method (or we could pick a different name) and calls it in that case rather than failing.

issue_title: Ensure dump.to_path|zip saves UTF-8 descriptor (ATM it is ascii)
issue_body:
First of all here is a standard of JSON: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7159#section-8.1
> 8.1.  Character Encoding
>   JSON text SHALL be encoded in UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32.  The default
   encoding is UTF-8, and JSON texts that are encoded in UTF-8 are
   interoperable in the sense that they will be read successfully by the
   maximum number of implementations; there are many implementations
   that cannot successfully read texts in other encodings (such as
   UTF-16 and UTF-32).

Attention here:
>   Implementations MUST NOT add a byte order mark to the beginning of a
   JSON text.  In the interests of interoperability, implementations
   that parse JSON texts MAY ignore the presence of a byte order mark
   rather than treating it as an error.

Second - python's  `json.dump[s]` save json file in `ascii` by default:
https://docs.python.org/3/library/json.html#basic-usage
> `ensure_ascii=True`

So does the DPP - saves descriptors in the `ASCII` despite the file encoding is `utf-8`:
https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/blob/0bc5276c2acd730fd765af99dbfdecbca9b1c46d/datapackage/package.py#L249
https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/blob/0bc5276c2acd730fd765af99dbfdecbca9b1c46d/datapackage/package.py#L269

As a result
`"title": "мамы"` becomes `"title": "\u043c\u0430\u043c\u044b"` after `dump.to_file`

@akariv @roll  Do you have any objections if I fix it ?

issue_title: (feature request) from solc import __version__
issue_body:
> * `py-solc` Version: x.x.x

that is my question - how to query that?

    from solc import get_solc_version
    print (get_solc_version())

is not the answer.

It is in 2 places - in [.bumpversion.cfg](https://github.com/ethereum/py-solc/blob/19f5877abc7152ca62f5e618e178dc9ee9883b05/.bumpversion.cfg#L2) and [setup.py](https://github.com/ethereum/py-solc/blob/19f5877abc7152ca62f5e618e178dc9ee9883b05/setup.py#L12) - please create some wrapper, perhaps like [this](https://github.com/pipermerriam/eth-testrpc/blob/9e2fe3cdc6043077cff113f5eeb9267e59c531d1/testrpc/__init__.py#L13)?

### What was wrong?

Nothing. All good.

But Ethereum's standards are changing too often, so I'd like to always [print version numbers](https://gitlab.com/electronDLT/chainhammer/blob/72398637198e381aa2c16518abe551ccda80b440/config.py#L37-43) for the ethereum toolchain - to [avoid troubles like this](https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/issues/808#issuecomment-385989701), I am going to [import specific versions](https://gitlab.com/electronDLT/chainhammer/blob/72398637198e381aa2c16518abe551ccda80b440/README.md#virtualenv-for-py3-dependencies-mostly-ethereum).

Current situation seems to be:

issue_title: Non-deterministic results on smart contract call with same input
issue_body:
* Version: 3.16.4
* Python: 3.5
* OS: Ubuntu 16.04


### What was wrong?

Executing the following smart contract with the following web3.py calls (adopted from the sample code on the Github page) yields two different outputs, with no apparent underlying cause.

The smart contract:
```
pragma solidity ^0.4.0;

contract TestContract
{
    bytes32 recorded_value;
    bytes32 recorded_salt; //Unused, but required for the problem to appear

    function TestContract(bytes32 value, bytes32 salt) public
    {
        recorded_value = value;
    }

    function getHash() public constant
        returns (bytes32)
    {
        return keccak256(recorded_value);
    }
}
```

The web3.py calls deploying and calling the smart contract:

```
#Based on the source code from https://github.com/pipermerriam/web3.py
import json
import web3

from web3 import Web3, HTTPProvider, TestRPCProvider
from solc import compile_source
from web3.contract import ConciseContract

w3 = Web3(TestRPCProvider())

def deploy_smart_contract(source_code_file_path, contract_name, constructor_arguments):
  with open(source_code_file_path) as f:
    commitment_helper_source_code = f.read()
  compiled_sol = compile_source(commitment_helper_source_code)
  contract_interface = compiled_sol['<stdin>:' + contract_name]
  smart_contract = w3.eth.contract(contract_interface['abi'], bytecode=contract_interface['bin'])
  tx_hash = smart_contract.deploy(transaction={'from': w3.eth.accounts[0]}, args=constructor_arguments)
  tx_receipt = w3.eth.getTransactionReceipt(tx_hash)
  smart_contract_address = tx_receipt['contractAddress']
  deployed_smart_contract = w3.eth.contract(contract_interface['abi'], smart_contract_address, ContractFactoryClass=ConciseContract)
  return deployed_smart_contract, smart_contract

def print_hex(char_array):
  output = "0x";
  for char in char_array:
    output += str("%02x" % ord(char))
  return output

contract_instance, contract = deploy_smart_contract('contract.sol', 'TestContract', {Web3.toBytes(123), Web3.toBytes(100)})

value = contract_instance.getHash()
print("Hash: " + print_hex(value))
```

The output of two executions of the above code:

```
(.venv-py3) andreas@***:/media/sf_temp$ python test.py
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:56] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 499
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:56] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 48
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:56] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 44
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:56] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 1350
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:56] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 107
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:56] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 391
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:56] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 391
Hash: 0x4cab01aa70c7d27ce12007ae744261ed3e0ee9162bc588f68526ea903ec2eb99
(.venv-py3) andreas@***:/media/sf_temp$ python test.py
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:58] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 499
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:58] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 48
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:58] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 44
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:58] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 1350
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:58] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 107
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:58] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 391
127.0.0.1 - - [27/Jan/2018 15:39:58] "POST / HTTP/1.1" 200 391
Hash: 0x75a12a7aab601952ef71dd6da71725eaa4eb7883096067546c8ae3f99cc9222e
```

Note the different hashes despite the inputs and the smart contract being the same. I posted some more details [here](https://ethereum.stackexchange.com/questions/37678/non-deterministic-result-of-simple-smart-contract), the most important likely being that removing the `keccak256` call in the smart contract shows the same problem, but is harder to provoke reliably.

### How can it be fixed?

I don't know whether this is a problem of web3.py, my use of it or my setup. I have no suggestions on how to fix it since I do not know what causes it.

issue_title: Bug in exclude() using contains
issue_body:
I have a query that includes a condition like the following:

```python
.exclude(hstore_field__contains={'key_name': 'value'})
```

In django-hstore 1.3.4 and earlier, this translated to the following SQL:

```sql
WHERE NOT ("app_model"."hstore_field" @> hstore(ARRAY['key_name'], ARRAY['value']) AND "app_model"."hstore_field" IS NOT NULL)
```

But starting in 1.3.5, it changed to the following:

```sql
WHERE NOT (("app_model"."hstore_field"->'key_name') = 'value' AND "app_model"."hstore_field" IS NOT NULL)
```

The latter breaks down when the hstore field is not null but doesn't contain the key.  It simplifies as follows:

> WHERE NOT (NULL = 'value' AND TRUE)
> WHERE NOT (NULL AND TRUE)
> WHERE NOT NULL
> WHERE NULL

Hence the condition ultimately evaluates as false and the record is excluded, even though we were trying to get records like this one that don't contain the specified key/value combination.  The SQL from earlier versions works correctly, because it doesn't throw a NULL into the boolean operations.

So what happens is: conda finds out that the linking doesn't seem to work for a file, it retries this file for 3.2 seconds, fails and then continues with the next file - which also fails. 

Now as you can see, the package is boost and it has 12475 files. This would go on for about 10-12hours, without saying anything to the user.

We could not really reproduce why the package was corrupted in the first place but it seems after we cleaned the cache it worked..

### Steps to Reproduce
```
conda install <a package with missing files>
```


## Expected Behavior

Conda should stop after the first verification error, maybe rollback and tell the user that verification failed.

issue_title: Custom deactivate scripts in etc/conda/deactivate.d/ cannot modify the PATH environment variable
issue_body:
On Windows with conda 4.2.9, I am attempting to set environment-specific environment variables by following [this documentation](http://conda.pydata.org/docs/using/envs.html#windows).

I've set up the following custom activate/deactivate scripts in the environment folder.
In `etc/conda/activate.d/env_vars.bat`:
```
set R_HOME=C:\Local\Programs\R-3.3.2
set R_USER=C:\Users\ENCPB\Documents\
set R_LIBS_USER=C:\Local\Programs\R_libs
set PATH=C:\Local\Programs\R-3.3.2\bin:%PATH%
```

In `etc/conda/deactivate.d/env_vars.bat`:
```
set R_HOME=
set R_USER=
set R_LIBS_USER=
set PATH=%PATH:C:\Local\Programs\R-3.3.2\bin:=%
```

`R_HOME`, `R_USER`, `R_LIBS_USER` are properly set and unset, but not `PATH`. It is properly set but the deactivate script leaves `C:\Local\Programs\R-3.3.2\bin` in the PATH.

In the global Anaconda `Scripts\deactivate.bat` script, the `PATH` environment variable is actually set **after** the custom `etc/conda/deactivate.d/` scripts are run. Any change custom scripts do to `PATH` is ignored.

issue_title: add_external_library only available at project level
issue_body:
I see that `add_external_library` is only available at the project level. How should one handle the case where different libraries that are named the same thing may be needed for different components within the project?

For example: Xilinx `unisim` and Aldec. Aldec supplies two pre-compiled unisim libraries, one for vivado and one for ISE.  If `moduleA` depends on the unisim for vivado, and `moduleB` depends on the unisim for ISE, it seems impossible to do this in the same project.

I hate that this is an issue at all -- I wish Aldec supplied everything wrapped into a single library, but perhaps the issue goes deeper than that.

Are there any similar cases with other vendors or silicon vendors?

One possible solution to this case is to allow setting the external library to be scoped similar to other configurations. Then you could set it at a global level (the default), and possibly override at the library/testbench/test case level.

issue_title: With "delete_aliases: True" but no aliases present, "close" doesn't close
issue_body:
I love the "delete_aliases" flag in curator 4. 
However, people should be aware that closing the indexes will fail if `delete_aliases` is set to `true` and there is no alias to delete. This is due to elasticsearch's behaviour: 
`curl -XDELETE host:port/some_index/_alias/_all`
throws an error when there was no alias associated with the `some_index`. It looks like "all" means "one to many" here. I expected a "zero to many" interpretation and was surprised.
This error will cause the `close`  operation to fail. 
(This remains true if `continue_if_exception: False` is set, because this will not force the current action to be continued, but the subsequent actions, as explained in https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/client/curator/4.0/option_continue.html)

issue_title: Enable gzip,deflate headers to utilize `http.compression`
issue_body:
Elasticsearch provides an `http.compression` setting for utilizing `gzip,deflate` as `accept_encoding`.

When using the `urllib3` Connection class, you may enable this manually as so:

```python
es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch(hosts=hosts,
                                 **connection_settings)

es.transport.headers.update(urllib3.make_headers(accept_encoding=True))
```

issue_title: [Output] Remove "list" output type

MultinomialHMM - how can I set prior emission probabilities?
issue_body: The init method of MultinomialHMM can get the prior of start probabilities and transition probabilities but not the emission probabilities. This may cause the association between state index in the model and states of the underlying model to be not consistent. E.g. let's say state 0 has p_emission of [0.1, 0.9] (corresponding to symbol 0 and 1, respectively), whereas state 1 has probability of emission [0.9, 0.1]. We would like to have emissions of 1's to be associated with state 0.

issue_title: [Discussion] Make app object importable
issue_body:
I know aiohttp is against singletons and global variables but I wanted to open a discussion to see what's the opinion on this.

-----------------

Right now we have `request` and `app` objects as MutableMappings so we can store data, instances of connectors (i.e. Redis, postgres, caching, etc.). on them.

If its something you have to use while the app is alive, you will store that something inside the app object doing `app['cache'] = MyCache`. Similarly, if there is data that needs to live through the request cycle, you will do `request['data'] = my_data`.

Problem is that the app object once you've bootstrapped the application, its only accessible through the `request` object with `request.app`. This for simple handlers/views is more than enough because everything happens in that function but, in case of very complex handlers there is no other way but propagating the `request` object everywhere. This is not ideal because you start coupling all your functions, classes, etc. to aiohttp flow of receiving the fat request object that contains the app with the objects needed to work with. Plus, you need to propagate this object to ALL parts of your code and its very painful both for coding and for testing.

My proposal is to make the app importable. Something like `from aiohttp import current_app` or something like that (can be discussed here). This way you could do something like:

```
from aiohttp import current_app

class MyService:
    def __init__(self):
        cache = current_app['cache']
```

This would also open the door of storing configuration in the app so it can be used anywhere.

I know it may go against some opinions but I sometimes miss a lot having objects easily accessible. Other frameworks do it:

- Flask (although Flask does it TOO much IMO)
- Django: `from django.core.cache import cache`, `from django import settings`, etc.
- Apistar although its quite different because of all the dependency injection and components.

So I'm just opening this to gather feelings/opinions around this and to see if I'm the only one feeling that way :)

issue_title: Relax attribute lookup in warning about old-styled middleware
issue_body:
For class-based middleware like
```
class Middleware:
    async def __call__(self, app, handler):
          ...
```
there is no `__name__` attr.
Moreover I could imaging other situations where `__name__` doesn't exists.
It breaks web server starting.

Let's change `middleware.__name__` to `repr(middleware)`.

Bugfix should be done in `2.3` branch.

issue_title: Notebook not printing intermediate values
issue_body:
I run the following code in ipython and get the expected result, a printed countdown of numbers and a final message:

```python
import time
import random

def countdown(s):
    """Print the remaining seconds."""
    while s:
        print("{}".format(s), end="\r")
        time.sleep(1)
        s -= 1

def run(s=5):
    """Return a winner from a pool given."""
    countdown(s)
    print("Done")
    

run()
# 4 3 2 1 Done
```

In the notebook for `ipykernel` > 4.2.2, the cell hangs until `'Done'` is printed without intermediate numbers.  I do not notice any output to the console as suggested in #111.

issue_title: JS script less-watch is installed with setup.py install
issue_body:
[less-watch](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/5.0.0/scripts/less-watch) is installed to bindir (such as `/usr/bin`) with `setup.py install`. According to @takluyver, it is [not desired](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/commit/75835e4ca8e45e71f49724abe41f07e2e6218401#commitcomment-21638624).

The reason for the installation is [a glob in `setup.py`](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/5.0.0/setup.py#L79) that takes all files from the `scripts` dir. Please either move that file somewhere else or change the glob to only include `pjoin('scripts', 'jupyter-*')`. Thanks

issue_title: Memory issue?
issue_body:
I've run into an issue with large matrices and memory. There seem to be two problems:

1) Memory isn't being released on successive calls of grad. e.g.

```
a = 10000
b = 10000
A = np.random.randn(a)
B = np.random.randn(b)

def fn(x):
    M = A[:, na] + x[na, :]
    return M[0, 0]
    
g = grad(fn)

for i in range(100):
    g(B)
```
is ramping up memory on each iteration.

2) Memory isn't being released during the backwards pass e.g.

```
k = 10
def fn(x):
    res = 0
    for i in range(k):
        res = res + np.sum(x)
    return res
g = grad(fn)
b = 200000
g(np.random.randn(b))
```
This seems to scale in memory (for each call) as O(k), which don't think is the desired behaviour. For b=150000 this effect does not happen, however.

issue_title: Make fill=False by default (for all functions)
issue_body:
It sounds like @tacaswell, @klauer, and I all agree on this. OK with everyone else?

Proximate Motivation: `restream` and `process` pass the documents to bluesky callbacks, which expect the *un-filled* documents that the RunEngine provides. Filled Documents cause them to blow up when the try to (redundant) fill them.

General Motivation: Filling is expensive and we shouldn't do it by default.

Is there any chance I can bait someone else into looking at this? @arkilic or @ericdill? I could use a break from databroker work for awhile, but this should get done soon.

issue_title: UnboundLocalError: local variable 'range' referenced before assignment
issue_body:
Python2 and Python produce a UnboundLocalError for the following


```
def foo():
    a = range(1, 10)
    range = a
    return range


foo()

print(range)
```

pyflakes doesnt see any problems with it.

This is because pyflakes is special casing all of the builtins, and normal rules are not being applied.  In this case, `range` is a local variable, and is not a builtin in that scope.

If we change `range` to `bar` in the above, pyflakes emits `undefined name 'bar'` for line 2, which isnt perfect, but is better than not detecting there is problem at all.

issue_title: Passing dictionary argument to BatchRunner
issue_body:
Not sure if it is an issue and not intended behaviour but nevertheless decided to notify about it.

When you pass a dictionary value into batch runner it only takes keys and not values. So you'll not get a cartesian product of your values. For example, if you pass something like this:

` 
class MoneyModel(Model):

    def __init__(self, n, grid_params):  # grid_params is intended to be a dictionary
        super().__init__(self)
        ...

def main():
    ...
    n_iters, n_steps = 5, 200
    params = {
        "grid_params": {"height": 10, "width": 10},
        "n": range(10, 500, 10)}
    batch = BatchRunner(MoneyModel, params,
        iterations=n_iters, max_steps=n_steps,
        model_reporters={"Gini": compute_gini})
    batch.run_all()
`


You'll get issue https://github.com/projectmesa/mesa/blob/master/mesa/batchrunner.py#L71, i.e. variable `kwargs` will be equal to `{'grid_params': 'width', 'n': 10}` (or any other dictionary key) instead of original value.

issue_title: letsencrypt-auto depends on sudo / no support for PREFIX?
issue_body:
(Or, "Dude, where's my sudo?")

As it stands, if you don't have `sudo`, you're toasted. The default in Debian is to not install `sudo` for bare-metal installations. As such, a Debian Jesse/stable minimalist installation doesn't have `sudo`, because there's a password set for `root`.

Should `letsencrypt-auto` depend on sudo? Or just say "Run me as root"? Or, optionally list the packages that need to be installed?

For completeness, this is the entire output from `letsencrypt-auto`:

```
indrora@zaibatsutel:~/letsencrypt$ ./letsencrypt-auto 
Bootstrapping dependencies for Debian-based OSes...
./letsencrypt-auto: 40: ./letsencrypt-auto: sudo: not found
```

Alternately, would a message of "sudo isn't there, using su -c '...'" be appropriate?

For documentation down the road for Letsencrypt, would love something that lays out the client usage like https://sslmate.com/help/automation and https://sslmate.com/help/getting_started where it's formatted in a step by step basis structure which is end user task oriented.

* Other Apache source/custom installs

This is as opposed to just listing the Letsencrypt client options etc.

issue_title: confluent-kafka-python resiliency to network errors
issue_body:
How resilient is python confluent-kafka to network errors? I am asking because I noticed two problems when the network between the python kafka client and the kafka brokers is interrupted for 10 minutes. 
I have a message producer and a message consumer (code below). 
The producer produces messages with a sequence number, the consumer receives those messages and print them on a file.

This is what I see when the network is down:
- On the producer I see delivery errors (code=_MSG_TIMED_OUT) reported by the on_delivery callback. I expected these messages to be flushed when the network was up again, but all the messages produced while the network was down are lost (see second consumer log). I thought the messages produced while the network is down are stored in an internal local queue (no exception is raised by the produce call), and they are all flushed when 1) the network is up again, and 2) a call to the flush method is made. This does not seem to be the case. 
- On the consumer I see one TIMED_OUT_QUEUE exception ~100 seconds after the network is down, and I see no messages received from that moment on, even after the network is up again. Thinking there was a problem with the state of the consumer I tried to reinitialize it (calling the consumer, unsubscribe, close, init and subscribe again) but this did not help. The consumer is able to consume messages again only if I restart the consumer program. The messages produced with the delivery errors are lost.

I am using python confluent-kafka 0.9.2 with librdkafka 0.9.2. The version of the kafka broker is 0.10.0.1.

**Producer Code:**

```
def produce(config, topic_name, retry_interval):
    def on_delivery(error, msg):
        if error is not None:
            logger.warning("An error was encountered while delivering a kafka message: %s", error)
    
    producer_config = config.copy()
    producer_config.update({
        'client.id': 'test-producer',
        'api.version.request': True,
    })
    
    logger.debug("Initializing producer ...")
    producer = confluent_kafka.Producer(producer_config)
    logger.debug("Producer initialized.")

    n = 1    
    while True:
        try: 
            value = {
                "sequence_no": n
            }
                
            logger.info("Producing message: '%s' ...", value)
            producer.produce(topic_name, json.dumps(value), callback=on_delivery)
            logger.info ("Message produced: '%s' ...", value)
            time.sleep(2)
            logger.debug("Flushing messages ...")
            producer.flush()
            logger.debug("Messages flushed.")
            n += 1
            time.sleep(2)
        except confluent_kafka.KafkaException as e:
            logger.warning("An error was encountered while producing a kafka message: %s", str(e.args[0]))
            logger.warning("Retrying producing kafka message ...")
            time.sleep(retry_interval)
```

**Consumer code:**

```
def consume(config, topic, poll_timeout, retry_interval):
    consumer_config = config.copy()
    consumer_config.update({
        'client.id': 'test-consumer',
        'group.id': 'test-consumer-group',
        'api.version.request': True,
        'default.topic.config': {
            'enable.auto.commit': False #default: True
        }         
    })
    
    logger.debug("Initializing consumer ...")                
    consumer = confluent_kafka.Consumer(consumer_config)
    logger.debug("Consumer initialized.")                
    subscribed = False
    
    while True:
        try:
            if not subscribed:
                consumer.subscribe([topic])
                logger.debug("Consumer subscribed to: %s",  topic)
                subscribed = True
                
            msg = consumer.poll(poll_timeout)           
            if msg is None:
                # poll timed out
                logger.debug("POLL TIMED OUT")
                continue            

            if not msg.error():
                logger.info("Message received: %s", msg.value())
            else:
                error_code = msg.error().code()
                if error_code == confluent_kafka.KafkaError._PARTITION_EOF:
                    continue
                 
                logger.error("An error was encountered while consuming kafka messages: %s", str(msg.error()))
                raise confluent_kafka.KafkaException(msg.error())
        except confluent_kafka.KafkaException as e:
            logger.warning("A possibly recoverable error was encountered while consuming kafka messages: %s", str(e.args[0]))
            logger.warning("Retrying consuming kafka messages ...")
            time.sleep(retry_interval)
            
            # reconnect
            if subscribed:
                logger.debug("Unsubscribing consumer ...")                
                consumer.unsubscribe()
                logger.debug("Consumer unsubscribed.")                
                subscribed = False
                
            logger.debug("Closing consumer ...")                
            consumer.close()
            logger.debug("Consumer closed.")                
            logger.debug("Reinitializing consumer ...")                
            consumer = confluent_kafka.Consumer(consumer_config)
            logger.debug("Consumer reinitialized.")
```

issue_title: Producer fails to use Kafka cluster's fault tolerance?
issue_body:
I have three brokers functioning as a Kafka cluster, so that my 'bootstrap.servers' is "198.18.130.12,198.18.130.13,198.18.130.14". It happens that of these, 198.18.130.14 is down (even though 198.18.130.12 and 198.18.130.13 are still up). I would expect that because Kafka is smart about redundancy within a cluster, I would still be able to talk to the two brokers that are still up, but the producer seems to really want to connect to the down broker, and only the down broker, as far as I can tell.

I get a call to my error_cb():
code: -195, name: _TRANSPORT, str: 198.18.130.14:9092/bootstrap: Connect to ipv4#198.18.130.14:9092 failed: Connection refused

Then eventually I get a call to my on_delivery callback:
code: -192, name: _MSG_TIMED_OUT

So it looks like my producer is dead in the water.

Is there something I should be doing differently in the configuration?

FWIW, the corresponding consumer also gets a -195/_TRANSPORT/"198.18.130.14:9092/bootstrap: Connect to ipv4#198.18.130.14:9092 failed: Connection refused" error, but I have a hunch that it does not regard this as a showstopper the way the producer does.

issue_title: `Error collecting output` for array of secondaryFiles
anonymous directory are allowed to only have a listing
issue_body:
According to my reading of http://www.commonwl.org/v1.0/Workflow.html#Directory

```
if d["class"] == "Directory" and ("listing" not in d or "basename" not in d):
                raise validate.ValidationException("Anonymous directory object must have 'listing' and 'basename' fields.")
```

is incorrect

https://github.com/common-workflow-language/cwltool/blob/master/cwltool/pathmapper.py#L59

@tetron Am I reading the spec wrong? Seems that if there is a listing and nothing else then "[t]he implementation must assign a unique identifier for the location field.", then the basename follows "If not provided, the implementation must set [the basename] based on the location field by taking the final path component after parsing location as an IRI."

This came up while debugging https://www.biostars.org/p/216220/ -- so perhaps the issue is that the reference implementation thinks this directory is anonymous when in fact it will have a real path in the output directory.

issue_title: QTile plugin has inconsistent or wrong identifiers
issue_body:
It is wrong to have qtile textbox widget class have the same name as main powerline class. Also word `timeout` (in arguments) usually refers to maximum time something should wait or, at least, time after which something should be run (once), not to the interval between two runs.

issue_title: Add open() and close() to SerialBase
issue_body:
The `SerialBase` should have the `open()` and `close()` Method implemented with a `raise NotImplementedError()` like this:

````python
class SerialBase(io.RawIOBase):
    # All the existing code ...

    def open(self):
        """Opens the Port"""
        raise NotImplementedError("This function must be implemented in the subclass")

    def close(self):
        """Closes the Port"""
        raise NotImplementedError("This function must be implemented in the subclass")
````     

This way it is immediatly clear that this functions must be implemented in subclasses and if somenone is looking at the code of `SerialBase` its obvious where these methods are doing.

issue_title: Pgen relpaths calculation
issue_body:
Currently, pgen uses the function _set_output_dir to calculate the relative ".." identifiers needed to get from a tool project file to the actual project file referenced in the yaml. It adds this calculation to a dictionary that will eventually get passed to tools, and all tools must  call a _fix_paths function to append these ".."s to all filenames. I think this could be simplified using python's os.path.relpath function. 

I think it is in our best interest to simplify these dictionaries that are passed around as much as possible.

You can see how it is currently calculated here:
https://github.com/project-generator/project_generator/blob/dev_v0.7/project_generator/project.py#L391

And how it is used here:
https://github.com/project-generator/project_generator/blob/dev_v0.7/project_generator/tools/uvision.py#L123

You can see an examples of os.path.relpath below:
![relpaths](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/12565767/8655983/71e0258c-295a-11e5-9642-d5bf4363d59f.png)

issue_title: PostgresNode.safe_psql(filename) does not detect syntax errors
issue_body:
It took me some time to discover that a syntax error was being not being reported by `node.safe_psql(filename='some.sql')`. I tracked it down to behavior in `psql`. It seems worth raising, though I'm not certain it is something that testgres is necessarily responsible for handling.

---

```
$ psql --version
psql (PostgreSQL) 9.6.4
```
and/or
```
$ psql --version
psql (PostgreSQL) 10.4
```

`psql` reports errors differently depending on how input is passed:

1. ```
   $ psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U cbandy -X -A -t -q -c 'whoops' -p 34751 postgres ; echo $?
   ERROR:  syntax error at or near "whoops"
   LINE 1: whoops
           ^
   1
   ```
2. N.B. exit code is zero here:
   ```
   $ psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U cbandy -X -A -t -q -f <( echo 'whoops' ) -p 34751 postgres ; echo $?
   psql:/dev/fd/63:1: ERROR:  syntax error at or near "whoops"
   LINE 1: whoops
           ^
   0
   ```
   
3. ```
   $ psql -h 127.0.0.1 -U cbandy -X -A -t -q -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -f <( echo 'whoops' ) -p 34751 
   postgres ; echo $?
   psql:/dev/fd/63:1: ERROR:  syntax error at or near "whoops"
   LINE 1: whoops
           ^
   3
   ```

issue_title: A model with multiple MoneyField sharing a single currency?
issue_body:
I have a model where there are multiple amounts, which must all be in a same single currency. I know I can just throw in multiple `MoneyFields` and ensure the consistency in `Model.clean`, but this would create unnecessarily many database fields, which I don't like. I could've also used plain and simple `DecimalField`s, but since I plan to use `Money` objects everywhere I want consistency in this regard (and don't want to construct those explicitly everywhere I need the field's value).

From what I understood from reading the source code, currently `MoneyField` always automatically creates paired `CurrencyField` and there's no control over this.

I wonder if a field type, or an extension of `MoneyField`'s API that'd use explicitly-created `CurrencyField` would be a good addition. I.e.:

    class Invoice(models.Model):
        currency = CurrencyField(...)
        total = MoneyField(..., currency_field_name="currency")
        paid = MoneyField(..., currency_field_name="currency")
        ...

In this scenario, to keep things simple and avoid unexpected bugs, assigning a value with a mismatching currency should probably result in an exception:

    # This should fail and raise ValueError (or a subclass of it)
    invoice = Invoice(currency=USD, total=Money(10, USD), paid=Money(0, EUR), ...)

    invoice = Invoice(currency=USD, total=Money(10, USD), paid=Money(0, USD), ...)
    invoice.paid = Money(5, USD)      # This is OK
    invoice.paid = Money(8.79, EUR)   # This should fail

I had a limited success by subclassing and overriding `contribute_to_class` to return a specially prepared `MoneyFieldProxy` ([gist with my kludge that happens to work for me](https://gist.github.com/drdaeman/5d1a948e829ceb78af7afce701378545)), but it only works seemingly correctly if `CurrencyField` is properly listed above any dependent fields.

What do you think? Is something like this a good idea?

Thanks!

issue_title: XML corruption issues for subjobs
issue_body:
Ganga is still having problems correctly falling back to a backup XML file or other solution when failing to load an XML for a subjob.

I've also spotted that the test for this is broken owing to accidentally badly named tests running out of order.

issue_title: Authenticated user should be allowed to browse his buckets by default
issue_body:
Kinto config:

```
[app:main]
use = egg:kinto

# Required by integration tests
kinto.flush_endpoint_enabled = true

# Required by basic auth
kinto.userid_hmac_secret = a-secret-string

# Allow bbrowsing all buckets
# kinto.bucket_read_principals = system.Authenticated

[server:main]
use = egg:waitress#main
host = 0.0.0.0
port = 8888
```

Starting server

```
./.venv/bin/pserve test/kinto.ini
Starting server in PID 3230.
serving on http://0.0.0.0:8888
```

Listing buckets:

```
$ http :8888/v1/buckets -a a:
HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Content-Length, Expires, Alert, Retry-After, Last-Modified, Total-Records, ETag, Pragma, Cache-Control, Backoff, Next-Page
Content-Length: 95
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2016 08:58:07 GMT
Server: waitress

{
    "code": 403, 
    "errno": 121, 
    "error": "Forbidden", 
    "message": "This user cannot access this resource."
}
```

This is quite surprising, as one might expect being able to at least list his own default bucket in there.

If you create a custom bucket, then you're suddenly allowed to browser buckets:

```
$ http PUT :8888/v1/buckets/foo -a a:
HTTP/1.1 201 Created
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Retry-After, Content-Length, Alert, Backoff
Content-Length: 154
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2016 09:03:09 GMT
Etag: "1456218189534"
Last-Modified: Tue, 23 Feb 2016 09:03:09 GMT
Server: waitress

{
    "data": {
        "id": "foo", 
        "last_modified": 1456218189534
    }, 
    "permissions": {
        "write": [
            "basicauth:a2125d3ab6f6b9509e1bc87932010af71cebfd50424e972934a0e4b5a1047f42"
        ]
    }
}

$ http :8888/v1/buckets -a a:        
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Content-Length, Expires, Alert, Retry-After, Last-Modified, Total-Records, ETag, Pragma, Cache-Control, Backoff, Next-Page
Cache-Control: no-cache
Content-Length: 53
Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
Date: Tue, 23 Feb 2016 09:03:17 GMT
Etag: "1456218189534"
Last-Modified: Tue, 23 Feb 2016 09:03:09 GMT
Server: waitress
Total-Records: 1

{
    "data": [
        {
            "id": "foo", 
            "last_modified": 1456218189534
        }
    ]
}
```

TL;DR: I think users should be able to list buckets they have access to by default.

issue_title: Take into account the new publish layout
issue_body:
With Pulp 2.12 the following changes to the publish layout will be introduced 
http://pulpproject.org/2016/11/14/yum-repo-layout-changes/

Those changes apply to RPMs and SRPMs only, DRPMs will stay where they are now in `drpms/`:
```
repo
├── drpms
├── Packages
└── repodata
````

So it is needed to identify and fix tests which rely on the file structure of published repos.

As for upgrade, it can verify for existing repositories that:
- if there was no re-publish => old structure is still in place
- if re-publish was an incremental one => there will be a mixture of the old and new structure, new recently added RPMs/SRPMs will be organized in a new structure.
- if re-publish was full => only new structure should be used.

Redmine story: https://pulp.plan.io/issues/1976

errors during `Schema`'s `schema` property method extremely difficult to debug
issue_body:
Errors in `@property def schema` (and probably any other `@property` in that class) do not yield useful tracebacks; instead, python "helpfully" falls back to `__getattr__` and then fails in there, which is extremely unhelpful.  This appears to be a specific case of https://github.com/tomchristie/django-rest-framework/issues/2108

Would it be possible to ditch the `__getattr_` use, or alternatively the `@property`s?

Traceback of what I'm hitting:
```
<ipython-input-2-8b3585dcab10> in <module>()
----> 1 res2 = schema.execute("query { subject_user {user_id} }", request_context=dict(_subject_user_id=1))

/usr/local/encap/python-2.7.7/lib/python2.7/site-packages/graphene/core/schema.pyc in execute(self, request, root, args, **kwargs)
    120
    121     def execute(self, request='', root=None, args=None, **kwargs):
--> 122         kwargs = dict(kwargs, request=request, root=root, args=args, schema=self.schema)
    123         with self.plugins.context_execution(**kwargs) as execute_kwargs:
    124             return self.executor.execute(**execute_kwargs)

/usr/local/encap/python-2.7.7/lib/python2.7/site-packages/graphene/core/schema.pyc in __getattr__(self, name)
     47         if name in self.plugins:
     48             return getattr(self.plugins, name)
---> 49         return super(Schema, self).__getattr__(name)
     50
     51     def T(self, _type):

AttributeError: 'super' object has no attribute '__getattr__'
```

issue_title: No exception message supplied
issue_body:
I'm new to GraphQL and Graphene.

After playing with the examples, I started to migrate an existing application to use GraphQL.

This seems to work:

```python
from graphene_django.types import DjangoObjectType
from graphene_django.filter import DjangoFilterConnectionField

from design_framework.core import models

import graphene

class HookNode(DjangoObjectType):
    class Meta:
        model = models.Hook
        interfaces = (graphene.Node, )

class Query(graphene.AbstractType):
    hook = graphene.Node.Field(HookNode)
    all_hooks = DjangoFilterConnectionField(HookNode)
```

When I add the following, it breaks (note how it is NOT referenced in the above code):

```python
class Node(DjangoObjectType):
    class Meta:
        model = models.Node
        interfaces = (graphene.Node, )
```

It ends with the following exception (when trying to open GraphiQL):

```
  File "/Users/basilfx/Desktop/trunk/application/backend-v2/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/types/typemap.py", line 73, in graphene_reducer
    assert _type.graphene_type == type
AssertionError
```

Pretty sure it has something to do with the class name `Node`. If I change it to `NodeNode`, it works. However, I stick with the same naming as per [README.md](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/README.md).

An error page resulting in  `AssertionError at /graphql No exception message supplied` does not say much, and I had to dig into it to find the cause (that seems unrelated).

issue_title: Automatically generate resolve functions
issue_body:
At the bottom of the official tutorial it says

>As you can see, GraphQL is very powerful but there are a lot of repetitions in our example. We can do a lot of improvements by adding layers of abstraction on top of `graphene-django`.

However, in the 1.0 blog post it says

>Ok, it’s hard to do a maintainable Python package based in the in the syntactic sugar magic behind the metaclasses.

I'm new to Python metaclasses and thought it would be a cool challenge to try and automatically add resolve functions for certain fields. I basically want to make `resolve_*` functions be automatically added in simple cases:

```
class Query(graphene.AbstractType):
    lesson = graphene.Field(LessonType, id=graphene.Int())

    # def resolve_lesson(self, args, context, info):
    #     This will get automatically added!
    #     id = args.get('id')

    #     if slug is not None:
    #         return Lesson.objects.get(id=id)

    #     return None
```

I tried to do this by defining my own metaclass inheriting from `AbstractTypeMeta`, and adding extra resolve functions to attrs before `AbstractTypeMeta` does its thing. Unfortunately due to the way the schemas are combined with `graphene.ObjectType`, I get a metaclass conflict. At this point I gave up as it seems like I'm either missing something or it will be a long headache if I continue.

My question is, are there any capabilities or plans to be able to do what I want to do?

By the way, very excited to start using GraphQL! I've only just discovered it today but it looks very promising.

issue_title: 'tensorboard.plugins.projector' has no attribute 'ProjectorConfig'
issue_body:
Referring to the test file:
https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard/blob/1.5/tensorboard/plugins/projector/projector_api_test.py

If `projector` is imported as:
```python
from tensorboard.plugins import projector
config = projector.ProjectorConfig()
```
accessing the `ProjectorConfig()` raises:
`AttributeError: module 'tensorboard.plugins.projector' has no attribute 'ProjectorConfig'`

It works fine if `projector` is imported from `tf.contrib`:
```python
from tensorflow.contrib.tensorboard.plugins import projector
```
---
```
tensorboard == 1.5.0
tensorflow == 1.5.0
Python == 3.6.3
Ubuntu == 17.10
```

issue_title: Create a module that exposes all first-party plugin summaries
issue_body:
A user who wishes to use the new-style summaries will currently have to write,
```py
from tensorboard.plugins.histograms import summary as histogram_summary

foo = histogram_summary.op(…)
```
(The import alias is necessary in the common case where the user wants to use summaries of multiple types.)

This is a significant increase in verbosity over the status quo, which only requires
```py
import tensorflow as tf

foo = tf.summary.histogram(…)
```

Per previous discussion, we'd like to keep the main `tensorboard` module a normal module (no `__init__` file) for readability and tooling purposes, but include a module `tensorboard.summary` that imports the summary `op` and `pb` files from individual plugins. The API would be
```py
from tensorboard import summary

foo = summary.histogram(…)
foo_pb = summary.histogram_pb(…)
```

issue_title: Proposal: New UI to show GPU Details
issue_body:
We’re looking to extend the TensorBoard user interface to display interesting GPU details for each node in a neural network.  The GPU Details that we plan to display focus on the usage of tensor cores.  Tensor cores enable deep learning applications to run significantly faster.

Tensorboard GRAPHS tab: When a node is selected, and when the new ‘GPU Details’ radio button is clicked in navigator panel, a new properties panel will appear to the right called GPU details.  This new panel will display GPU details for that node:

- Percentage bar showing percentage of kernels using tensor cores
- Percentage bar showing percentage of duration using tensor cores
- Detailed kernel information, including kernel name, call counts, and durations.

Please review two new user interface elements on the GRAPHS tab:

- New radio button in navigator panel on left hand side, and 
- New ‘GPU Details’ properties panel on right hand side.

Notes:

- The nodes themselves will be shaded based upon the usage of tensor cores.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/35349680/42354874-bc7a37bc-807e-11e8-9bd4-0637529f3ea1.png)


Question: Is this in line with your ideas on how to display GPU Details for nodes in TensorBoard?  Any guidance here would be appreciated.

Thanks

issue_title: Add support for input format of 'feather' (feature request)
issue_body:
Feature request: Add support for reading files of format 'feather'.  Here is the repo for it: https://github.com/wesm/feather

Use case: 'feather'  files are an interop format for data files for doing data science between R and Python.  It allows one to preserve column type information within the metadata of the 'feather' file.  However, it is not easy to peek inside of them to see the contents.  As this is where `csvkit` excels as a tool, it would be nice to have support to point `in2csv` at a feather file and get CSV out for quick inspections.

Thank you for this great tool,
Matt

issue_title: certbot certificates should support sorting by (validity period, certificate name, reverse-tld)
issue_labels: [{'id': 210831837, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20cert%20management', 'name': 'area: cert management', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 261409142, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20renewal', 'name': 'area: renewal', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 200596079, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20ui%20/%20ux', 'name': 'area: ui / ux', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 276239890, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/easy-fix', 'name': 'easy-fix', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 146954789, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '00ccff', 'default': True}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/Good%20Volunteer%20Task', 'name': 'Good Volunteer Task', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': False}, {'id': 175439992, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/low%20priority', 'name': 'low priority', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
I'm not sure what the current sort order represents, but it's a bit odd

Being able to sort by validity is helpful for figuring out what's expiring soon.
Being able to sort by certificate name is helpful because it's an easier thing to understand than the random sort that's currently deployed.

fwiw, I currently have:
```
b.example.com (valid 58 days)
a.example.com (valid 89 days)
c.example.com (valid 58 days)
n.example.com (valid 58 days)
example.net (valid 63 days)
l.example.com (valid 58 days)
```

It clearly isn't sorted by name (b/a), by validity (58, 89, 58), nor tld.

Sorting by each of those could be useful.

Expected for validity:
```
a.example.com (valid 89 days)
example.net (valid 63 days)
b.example.com (valid 58 days)
c.example.com (valid 58 days)
n.example.com (valid 58 days)
l.example.com (valid 58 days)
```

Expected for certificate:
```
a.example.com (valid 89 days)
b.example.com (valid 58 days)
c.example.com (valid 58 days)
example.net (valid 63 days)
l.example.com (valid 58 days)
n.example.com (valid 58 days)
```

For tld, I think the algorithm should be:
sort by by each leaf, resorting by leafs to the right.

```
a.example.com (valid 89 days)
b.example.com (valid 58 days)
c.example.com (valid 58 days)
l.example.com (valid 58 days)
n.example.com (valid 58 days)
example.net (valid 63 days)
```

issue_title: CentOS 7 Python Conflict
issue_body:
If you're having trouble using Certbot and aren't sure you've found a bug or
request for a new feature, please first try asking for help at
https://community.letsencrypt.org/. There is a much larger community there of
people familiar with the project who will be able to more quickly answer your
questions.

## My operating system is (include version):
CentOS 7

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
OS Package Manager, from EPEL

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
yum install epel-release
yum install awscli certbot certbot-dns-route53

....   file /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/s3transfer/__init__.py conflicts between attempted installs of python-s3transfer-0.1.10-8.el7.noarch and python2-s3transfer-0.1.10-1.el7.noarch ....

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
It seems certbot-dns-route53 plugin cannot currently be installed alongside the awscli on CentOS 7. This seems to be due to conflicts in python packages from CentOS base and Epel. I'm unsure who can fix/who is responsible for this as it's not clear if it's a Certbot issue or a repo issue. It seems to be related to this https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1578071

issue_title: memory leak in lz4.frame.decompress
issue_body:
```
> python --version
Python 3.6.3
> pip freeze | fgrep lz4
lz4==0.18.1
```

I'm encountering an issue where when I run the script below, I can see the memory increasing.  At the end, the memory using is ~4GB looking at `htop`.

```
import lz4.frame
import tracemalloc
import time


tracemalloc.start()

input_data = 'a' * 1024 * 1024
compressed = lz4.frame.compress(input_data.encode('utf-8'))

prev_snapshot = None

for i in range(5000):
    decompressed = lz4.frame.decompress(compressed)

    if i % 100 == 0:
        snapshot = tracemalloc.take_snapshot()
        top_stats = snapshot.statistics('lineno')

        print(" top: {}".format(top_stats[0]))

        if prev_snapshot:
            top_stats = snapshot.compare_to(prev_snapshot, 'lineno')
            print("diff: {}".format(top_stats[0]))

        print()
        time.sleep(0.5)

        prev_snapshot = snapshot

print('done')
time.sleep(10)

issue_title: UPnP exception on next launch after unclean shutdown
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

In environments that use upnp for external port mapping abruptly terminating the raiden process (i.e. `kill -9`, power loss etc.) may leave an upnp port mapping behind which will cause a `ConflictInMappingEntry` exception on next launch in [`upnpsocket.py:63`](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/network/upnpsock.py#L63).

## Solution

Check if an upnp mapping exists before trying to register a new one.

## Tasklist

- [ ] Implement check for existing upnp mappings before registering a new one

issue_title: validation lost in postgreSQL
issue_body: Validation is getting lost during the flush/commit process in postgres. The before flush routine runs the validation normally, and validation errors are trapped and saved on the validation. But, when handle_before_exec executes (where ValidationError is normally raised by the validation sequence), target._sav_validate does not yield the desired _sav_ent_exec_count of 0, which triggers the final check and exception raise.

issue_title: PyTRiP generated CTX cubes cannot be loaded by TRiP98
issue_body:
```
import pytrip as pt

c = CtxCube()
c.read("shieldhit/res/TST000/TST000000.ctx)
c.set_byte_order("little")
c.write("TST000000.ctx")
```

problem is, that the written header file says ```version 1.2``` which TRiP will not load. Setting this to ```version 1.4``` fixes this, and all loads fine by TRiP98 (byte order is correct too, btw).

issue_title: Problem with pypi entry -- has underscores instead of dashes
issue_body:
At pypi, the software is listed as "django_polymorphic" (with underscores) instead of "django-polymorphic" (with hyphens), which means that the pypi entry doesn't match what you see when you install via pip ( "django-polymorphic==1.3" )


This has no impact on installing with `pip` because it handles the redirect fine, but does cause some less resilient software to complain, for example django extensions pipchecker:
```
# manage.py pipchecker
django-polymorphic 1.3           release is not on pypi (check capitalization and/or --extra-index-url)
```

issue_title: Confusing on parameter, definition, and global variable.
issue_body:
Suppose in notebook, we have

```
fname = 'a.txt'
```
already defined, then

```
%run --fname b.txt

input: fname
```
would actually take `a.txt` because `fname` is not defined as parameter, and a value already exists. We should either

1. stop using global variables ( #690 ) so that `fname` is undefined when it is not defined in `parameter`.
2. check for unused variable and notify user that `--fname b.txt` is not used because `fname` is not defined in `parameter`.

issue_title: Question: start concurrent jobs with time intervals in between
issue_body:
Same example as #926 but instead of worrying about disk space we worry here the download bandwidth balance. The workflow is again pasted here:

```
[1]
input: for_each = ['files_sra'], group_by = 1, concurrent = True
output: files_sra
download: 
  {url}/{_files_sra}

[2]
output: files_bam
run:
 sratools-dump ${_input} ${_output}
```

Apparently we cannot use very large `-j` for step 1 due to band-width limit. But if we use eg, `-j 1` then after download step 1, the `sratools` decryption step 2 will take long time, and the workflow will not download anything. This is a waste of resource. 

So ideally we want something like this:

```
1st file download -> 1st file decryption &  2nd file download -> 2nd file decryption & 3rd file download ...
```

How can we use SoS existing features to achieve this?

issue_title: Upload all wheels that we build in Travis.
issue_body:
Now that we're building all of the wheels every time Travis runs (as of #852), we should upload all of the wheels somewhere (where?). That way, if we're trying to figure out when a bug first appeared, we can programmatically iterate through the commit history, install ray from each wheel, and see when the bug appeared. It also would allow people to use the latest features more easily without waiting for the next release.

We would need to do the following.
1. Figure out where to store the wheels.
2. Make sure to only upload them from builds on the master branch and not from PRs.

issue_title: EXTRA BYTES: Scale, offset, raw values and data types
issue_body:
I'm reading a LAS file with some EXTRA BYTES and I noticed that accessing them with `inFile.<extrabytesvaluename>` (lower-case) returns the raw value arrays. The [LAS Specification 1.4](http://www.asprs.org/wp-content/uploads/2010/12/LAS_1_4_r13.pdf) pages 24-26, describes the format of the EXTRA BYTES VLR, and it mention that they may have associated scales and offsets, controlled by the `options` field inside the VLR (see Table 25), and that the type is controlled by the `data_type` field (see Table 24).

So, to be coherent with the rest of the package, I think the raw values should be accessed using `inFile.<Extrabytesvaluename>` (captioned) and the scaled value with offset applied should be available through `inFile.<extrabytesvaluename>` according with the controlling bits set in the `option` field (like it is done with `inFile.X` and `inFile.x`). The correct type can also be deduced from the `data_type` fields (chars, ints, floats, up to vectors of size 3), returning the appropriate vector.

Do you plan to implement this idea anytime soon? If someone give me some pointers to where to start I can try to adapt the code to handle the EXTRA BYTES in the way I described here.

In focussingScan.py you will find 3 functions

The 2nd and 3rd functions perform focal scans by calling `Propagation()` which is based on the example in manual section 5.7

2. `Focus_Scan()` performs a focus scan calling makefield() on every loop

While I'm happy to code around this, I'm completely lost in trying to identify the source of the problem

issue_title: Stacker: `check_data_identical=True` fails to load generated file
issue_body:
Not sure if this a new issue after introducing fixes for #454, or due  to changes in dask, or existed before.

Basically we are not able to load data from the newly created file while stacker is still running, actual file is alright after stacker process finishes, my guess is that something is holding up netcdf file from being fully closed

Error occurs here:

https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/a256de335d19d32abfd740f4ee3a9fddbda77066/datacube_apps/stacker/stacker.py#L171

Even though we have already closed the file (and even renamed it to final destination name)

https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/a256de335d19d32abfd740f4ee3a9fddbda77066/datacube_apps/stacker/stacker.py#L164-L167

File is not accessible to external tools at this point either, calling something like `ncdump -h` on the file while paused in debugger generates output like this:

```
HDF5-DIAG: Error detected in HDF5 (1.10.1) thread 140735772541824:
  #000: H5F.c line 586 in H5Fopen(): unable to open file
    major: File accessibilty
    minor: Unable to open file
  #001: H5Fint.c line 1305 in H5F_open(): unable to lock the file
    major: File accessibilty
    minor: Unable to open file
  #002: H5FD.c line 1839 in H5FD_lock(): driver lock request failed
    major: Virtual File Layer
    minor: Can't update object
  #003: H5FDsec2.c line 940 in H5FD_sec2_lock(): unable to lock file, errno = 35, error message = 'Resource temporarily unavailable'                         
    major: File accessibilty
    minor: Bad file ID accessed
```

This strongly suggests that `nco.close()` doesn't fully close the file at this point, probably because of some references still being kept around, probably within `dask`.

issue_title: Make tests self-contained
issue_body:
We currently have some tests that are not self-contained-- for example, this works:
```
(for test in parsl/tests/test_checkpointing/test*; do nosetests -vx $test || exit; done ) ; echo $?
```
but this fails:
```
nosetests -xv  parsl/tests/test_checkpointing
```
The tests should be self-contained such that this isn't required, and each test can be run individually to determine if it passes.

issue_title: Particular Unplug-Plug sequences causes port freeze-up and thread hogs CPU
issue_body: If you reset the COMMS processor as it is being started up and enumerating over USB,   then the script can get into a state where it no longer recognizes the batlab on a particular serial port, and CPU usage goes up. It appears that the batlab object thread and associated channel object threads go away.

.studioml folder not being created from pypi
pytorch converted AvgPool test case with wrong pads size
issue_body:
@houseroad 
https://github.com/onnx/onnx/tree/master/onnx/backend/test/data/model/pytorch-converted/test_AvgPool3d
pads in graph is [0, 0, 0] not [0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]. For 3d input, pads size should be 6.

I have not checked similar cases (other AvgPool3d and MaxPool3d). Please take a look.

issue_title: mprof run script with command line arguments
issue_body: When using the `mprof run <script>` syntax its not possible to use command line arguments for the process as mprof interprets them instead. My current workaround is to modify `p = subprocess.Popen(program)` to use ` shell=True` and pass the script arg as a string with additional arguments. Maybe adding an command line option for using `shell=True` would be useful?

issue_title: Check for glue records when needed
issue_body:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7489#section-7.1

i.e. for

```
$ dig +short _dmarc.nhn.no TXT
"v=DMARC1\; p=none\; rua=mailto:dmarc@nhn.no,mailto:dsm1h58m@ag.dmarcian.com\; ruf=mailto:dmarcfull@nhn.no"
```
ag.dmarcian.com is external to nhn.no, so it needs to specify that it will accept reports for nhn.no this way:
 
```bash
$ dig +short nhn.no._report._dmarc.ag.dmarcian.com TXT
"v=DMARC1"
```

issue_title: markline 标记点不显示标注

```python
def test_line_user_define_markpoint():

        "商家A",

        clothes_v1,
        mark_point=["average", {"coord": ["裤子", 10], "name": "这是我想要的第一个标记点"}],

    line.add(
        "商家B",
        CLOTHES,
        clothes_v2,
        is_smooth=True,
        mark_point=[{"coord": ["袜子", 80], "name": "这是我想要的第二个标记点"}],

```

issue_title: Return from pixiedust.enableJobMonitor() spelled incorrectly
issue_body:
Using Version 1.1.9

I ran into this while testing something else, I figured I'd let you all know.  I can't imagine this would be a high priority issue.

### Expected behavior
For the command, `pixiedust.enableJobMonitor()`
The response printed to std out should be:
> Succe**ss**fully enabled Spark Job Progress Monitor

### Actual behavior
The actual response is:
> Succe**s**fully enabled Spark Job Progress Monitor

_(There is a missing 's')_
### Steps to reproduce the behavior
Send the commands:
```python
import pixiedust
pixiedust.enableJobMonitor()
```

issue_title: Add printsettings command?
issue_body:
I submitted this to Django core, but they tend to be very conservative and don't seem too excited about adding this to Django core and even suggested that it might be better for django-extensions:

https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/17037#comment:6

If there is interest, I can work this into a django-extensions change and submit a pull request. 

Marc
